column3: Each proton contains three tiny **quark** particles bound together.
An electron is the most common **lepton** in matter.
The Higgs **boson** gives other particles their mass.
A **neutrino** passes through planets without hitting anything.
A **photon** is a single particle of light energy.
Every **fermion** obeys the Pauli exclusion principle strictly.
A cosmic **muon** decays within two microseconds on average.
Physicists still search for the elusive **graviton** particle.
When matter meets **antimatter**, both annihilate into pure energy.
A cooled **superconductor** carries current without any resistance.
Quantum **entanglement** links two particles across vast distances.
**Decoherence** destroys quantum states through environmental interaction.
In **superposition**, a particle exists in many states simultaneously.
Quantum **tunneling** lets electrons cross impassable barriers.
Distant galaxies show a strong **redshift** in their light.
A star moving toward us produces a clear **blueshift**.
Astronomers measure stellar distance using **parallax** angles.
A black hole hides a **singularity** at its center.
Mass warps the fabric of **spacetime** around it.
Relativity predicts time **dilation** near the speed of light.
Every particle traces a **worldline** through spacetime.
A sphere is a curved two-dimensional **manifold**.
Salt acts as the **solute** dissolved in water.
Rocket fuel needs a powerful **oxidizer** to burn properly.
A **reductant** donates electrons during a chemical reaction.
Slow cooling causes **crystallization** of the dissolved minerals.
**Electrolysis** splits water into hydrogen and oxygen gas.
**Stoichiometry** tells chemists the exact reactant ratios needed.
Carbon has a **valence** of four in organic molecules.
**Fluorine** is the most reactive element in nature.
Atoms share electrons in a **covalent** chemical bond.
Table salt forms through a strong **ionic** attraction.
Metal atoms sit neatly in a crystal **lattice**.
Each electron occupies a specific atomic **orbital** shape.
Red light has a longer **wavelength** than blue light.
Two waves create **interference** patterns on the screen.
Light bends around small slits through **diffraction**.
A straw looks bent due to light **refraction** in water.
Honey has much higher **viscosity** than water.
A falling rock converts potential energy into **kinetic** energy.
The engine loses much energy as **thermal** radiation.
A **capacitor** stores electrical charge between two plates.
The **resistor** limits current flowing through the circuit.
Electrons flow through a closed **circuit** carrying current.
A battery provides a steady **voltage** to the bulb.
Magnetic **flux** changes when the coil rotates quickly.
The lens has a **focal** length of fifty millimeters.
The reaction releases heat, lowering the system's **enthalpy**.
**Plutonium** is a heavy radioactive element used in reactors.
**Transcription** copies DNA into messenger RNA molecules.
DNA **replication** occurs before every cell division.
Eye color is a visible **phenotype** trait in humans.
The **genotype** encodes the organism's inherited information.
Each parent passes one **allele** of every gene.
**Meiosis** produces cells with half the chromosome count.
**Mitosis** divides one cell into two identical copies.
Sperm and egg are specialized **gamete** cells.
A fertilized **zygote** grows into a whole organism.
The **embryo** develops rapidly during the first weeks.
A **synapse** passes signals between two brain neurons.
The **axon** carries electrical impulses away from the neuron.
Each **dendrite** receives signals from neighboring cells.
Serotonin is a **neurotransmitter** that affects mood.
Stress floods the body with **cortisol** hormone quickly.
Fear triggers an **adrenaline** rush through the bloodstream.
Every living **organism** needs energy to survive.
Bread provides a quick **carbohydrate** source of energy.
Cells burn **glucose** to power their daily activities.
DNA is built from a four-letter **nucleotide** alphabet.
The **ribosome** reads RNA and builds protein chains.
Each plant cell contains a green **chloroplast** organelle.
Exercise can speed up your resting **metabolism** noticeably.
Insulin is a **hormone** that regulates blood sugar.
The thyroid **gland** controls your metabolic rate.
A dangerous **pathogen** spreads through contaminated drinking water.
Vaccines train the body against a harmless **antigen**.
A rotating **pulsar** emits regular radio pulses.
A distant **quasar** shines brighter than entire galaxies.
A massive star ends life in a bright **supernova**.
Scientists discovered another Earth-like **exoplanet** last year.
